channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for. — 136 replies — 544.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

Open thread • Replies: 136 • Updated: 2026-06-23 16:15 UTC

OP

If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

LINK is a $5.7B token on $57.5M of annual fees. That’s ~99x revenue, and it has been flat for years, straight through every “banks are coming” cycle. The promise was always that adoption converts into value. It hasn’t. It’s been “soon” since 2020.

So here’s the question no diehard wants to answer:

What fee number, by what date, would make you sell? If LINK still earns ~$57M in 2028, is the thesis wrong, or is it still “soon”? If you can’t name the number and the date, you’re not investing in Chainlink. You’re believing in it. And belief has no exit.

Early replies (first 3)

62382825
never. Its not about the link token, its just about the hope for the future it gives. That is what keeps me going, even if its a delusion. Thus I will never sell, because as long as I hold the token that fantastical delusion can still be real in my mind

62382842
chatgpt post

62382990
>>62382842
OP is a mentally ill faggot

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62396382
jeet marines been really quiet since this dropped

https://youtu.be/26N4u0ihIT4?t=12
https://x.com/taodaily_io/status/2064680225577804267

62396420
>s-s-samefag
>w-w-why do you care???
>y-y-you're crying, not me
the insecure little fagit always accuses of you of samefagging, always thread stalks at least 3-4 different threads, and he does that for almost 10-12 hours 4-5 days per week, it is insane really
he took a small hiatus at the beginning of the year, when sentiment was utter dogshit and he hadn't found the new deadline to shill yet, but then returned at full force and has been going nonstop ever since
I'm still not sure if this is a case of legitimate mental ilness or paid shilling, because I honestly fail to see the value of aggresively shilling LINK on /biz/ of all places, unless the team is in ultra desperation mode

62396430
>>62396355
upload the full onne that was 3 hours long, this is the vid where i got my screenshot, '
sergey was on the right side...

this is from the same day tho...

ccant timestamp it since chainlink cut it out.

62396574
>>62396420
It takes a few dozen posts to get as triggered as you are right now, meaning you clearly just switched IDs.


>>62396430
According to this fuddie thread https://warosu.org/biz/thread/S61013113, the "15 years" bs came from this talk: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=x_D1MM_0EGQ
Go ahead and try to find it in there.
She says something vague about production being "a long ways away" in that talk, but somehow the fuddies turned that into 15-20 years.

62396650
>It is literally an entrenched jewish rent seeking project minted on a completely arbitrary blockchain which INCREASES frictions intentionally for no reason to collect a fee, and it is totally unnecessary and 100x slower and more expensive than its competitors to use.
At this point there are years worth of docs, threads, talks and even memes explaining this stuff. You don't have to like LINK, but at least understand what you're criticizing instead of throwing random FUD at the wall and hoping something sticks.
Price action is trash, yes. The team dumps tokens, yes. LINK has been a painful hold with massive opportunity cost. There is no denying that. But you should seriously decouple price FUD (totally valid) from tech FUD (makes you sound retarded).

62396660
>>62396650
>still emphasizing WORDS
>moar larp
funny how you're hitting every thread lmao
back up that 100x figure on cost and speed, faggot

62397187
>>62396650
>But you should seriously decouple price FUD (totally valid) from tech FUD (makes you sound retarded).

comical. what will it take for you to realize you don't have a piece of ownership of the tech company?

62397245
>>62396341
>>62396420
What about >>62395971
>>62396233
verbose xrp shill and adem in every single thread? these guys claim to not hold the token and yet have been spamming the board for years now, adem since 2021, verbose xrp shill seems like he really showed up around 2023 or so but hard to say for sure. Any thoughts on them? Wouldn't you say its more pathetic to strictly post fud in threads of tokens you don't even own?

62397794
>>62397187
>what will it take for you to realize you don't have a piece of ownership of the tech company?
I realize that.
Chainlink is a cybersecurity company, the security being driven by cryptography and economic incentives. Without the token the entire thing is not better than https. If these peopld truly wrote code for over ten years, only to build useless tech that already exists in other form.
Cryptography is nothing new. Cyprography applied in a decentralized manner, with economic incentives (staking/slashing) is innovative and revolutionary.
If it really is a scam, then it was a very elaborate and convncing one, and I will be willing to admit I was retarded and wrong. If it's not a scam and my thesis is correct then a lot of people on this board will rope. I don't really care if you buy it or not. I don't know or care about you, and you definitely don't have enough money to move the needle on the price.
All I am asking you is to simply take a week and study the protocol before making nonsensical claims.

62397819
>>62397794
>Chainlink
>Decentralized
Lmao. Go run a node and try to get whitelisted to actually being able to get it used and then come back to us.

62397938
>>62397819
>1pbtid
>phoneposting with capitalzations of acronyms
Hi verbose xrp shill I see you are hopping around id's in this thread.

62398220
>>62397819
>Chainlink
>Decentralized
Decentralized does not have to mean the cypherpunk fantasy of "banks aren't needed and everyone is a sovereign individual." It's largely a matter of semantics.
Decentralized simply means that the nodes participating in the network are independent of one another.
If I tell you to watch a football game and report the result back to me in exchange for money, it's difficult to guarantee that you're telling the truth.
If I tell you and another person—whom you don't know—to watch the same game and report the result back to me, my chances of learning the true result increase, because the two of you would have to find each other, coordinate, and risk not getting paid.
Now imagine I tell 20 people to do the same, and on top of that, each person must deposit $1,000 to be eligible to participate and earn money. Nobody has an incentive to lie, because doing so would put their deposit, any future payments for their service, and their reputation as football-game reporters—all of which generate income for them—at risk. That's a decentralized oracle network.
This is the meaning of decentralization and what Chainlink is building - consensus without a chain. Consensus as a service.
I'm done spoon feeding for today. Hope that was clear and helpful to all the perplexed and confused anons here.

62398292
>>62398220
Is McDonald's decentralized because they have 5 employees that I can ask what the price of a big Mac is?

62398491
>>62396382
"token holders don't get any revenue (pause for the clips...) at this point"
>at this point
it's a trick.

62400097
>>62397938
Hi biepum, monitoring the threads I see.
Hows that flippening doing?

62400234
>>62400097
>samefagging fuddie with a fresh new ID complaining about linkies being in link threads

What the fuck is wrong with you.

62400297
>>62400234
>Every post that triggers me is made by the same poster.
Mind breaking heavy bags

62400329
>>62400297
You really have no idea how insanely obvious you are, do you?

62400348
>>62398292
kek
the entire thesis of Chainlink btfo in a single post

62400559
>>62400329
You are deranged lmao, its hilarious how you think you are always talking to 1 person.

62400655
>>62400559
This is a dead board, this thread has been up for almost a week now.
Yet you keep actively seeking out Link threads and then crying about all the "Link shills" you see in them. All the while pretending it's a normal thing to do.

It's not, and you stick out more than ever.

62400679
>dead board
>retarded shill keeps necro bumping his dead shill thread
>hurrrr why does the entire board shit on me?? you're all samefags1!!1!1
stop giving him (You)s and don't forget to sagepost frens

62400691
>>62400679
>shill thread
oof, might wanna check out the OP, samefagboy.

62400897
>>62400655
I just noticed you spamming the thread bieps, just hopped in. Hit you with the reminder of the flippening. You verbally shit yourself again. The regular.

62400962
>>62382813
>white male

no thanks

62401040
>>62400897
>muh flippening
we know you're a xerpie, yes

62401056
>>62400962
Russians arent white

62401136
>>62401056
niggers aren't black

62401340
>still going at it for 5 days
removing IP count was a mistake

62401653
Rome wasn't built in a day.

item.title: NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!! THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!! SOMEONE MUS… — 141 replies — 439.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!! THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!! SOMEONE MUS…

Open thread • Replies: 141 • Updated: 2026-06-23 16:08 UTC

OP

NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!!

THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!!

SOMEONE MUST PUT A STOP TO THIS NOW!!!!!!!!!!!! REDDIT ARMY RISE UP!!!!!!!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62375140
this is entirely sustainable, you have EDS if you disagree

62375150
>>62375140
Short it then, stupid nigger

62375163
>company called "Space Exploration Technologies"
>run by the first trillionaire

>you STILL didn't buy any of their stock

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398185
>>62398178
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're part of the permanent underclass

62398188
HAHAHAHAHA

62398204
>>62381795
He spends much more than 1 million a day, per his company income statement

62398211
>>62398185
>Collapse the dollar
>Now everyone is a millionaire :)

62398213
>>62381795
wtf is mann._michael13 on about?

62398226
bros im worried OP will tie the noose after getting pumped and dumped, we need to be much more reassuring to our friends that mistakes happen and we can overcome mistakes if we work together. Don't let op be another hanged man in the bathtub meme.

62398877
>>62392029
I hate the French

62398970
>>62398090
This animu was a fun watch. Hope you enjoyed it too schadenfreude bro

62398978
>>62375128
>2pbtid

62399080
>>62375150
Bet.

Guess my gains.

62399090
>>62375128
You were saying?

62399160
>>62375140
Well, think of it like this. The right half is all the economic activity that having a jetliner enabled for humanity. Space is vaster still than the air. Space can do what air can do, and more, we can get resources in space. So you have to imagine that the total economic potential of space can be as great if not greater than the economic activity from air travel.
Spacex is the closest of all companies to making it so that the biggest cost to sending something to space is just the fuel to do it. Can they achieve that? If so, then they have a the jetliner of space travel. It would take tens if not hundreds of billions, and at least a decade if not 2 or 3 to replicate what spacex has done, from scratch. Most of their competitors aren't starting from scratch, but they are way behind. If you wanted it done quickly, it would be very expensive, and perhaps not even possible to buy the capability that spacex presents itself capable of delivering.

62399168
>>62375128
The one cow village of OP.

62399196
>>62375128
It is absolutely uncanny how /biz/ calls the top like this.
Anons like OP are like a inverse oracles.

62399203
>>62399196
not really it was kinda obvious
its priced like a top 10 company but makes no money. Even it does start to make top 10 company profit the price wont go anywhere so yeah

62399214
Rip to all the jeets falling for elon scam. I guess your villages are going to starve in the next few months

62399292
>>62399203
Yeah you could expect spacex to go down, but the timing is always tied to gloat posting.
>OH NO NO NO
>YOU’RE SO DUMB FOR BEING BEARISH
>HA HA HA HA
You could almost set puts to this.

62399317
SPCX will drop to at least the IPO price in the near term. I wouldn't even consider buying it until that happens.

62399364
>>62375784
>You could have 10x or 100x real estate
>but you don't want to make it up into outer space

62399387
>>62375128
The absolute STATE of OP...

>>62398185
What the fuck is the purpose of these bots? Why is this.shot.literally in every single thread in this board? How come jannies and mods do nothing about this spam?

62399575
>>62399160
Your first mistake was assuming spacex is a space company.

62400470
this is unironically going below 50. Retards who bought this and were shilling it here for a month finally got what they deserved. Even my fucking bank was shilling this scam to me. Remember it's better to miss out on too many oppotunities than buy too many scams

62400488
>>62375163
I love how lefties constantly do shit that nerfs their prosperity. ESG, DEI, Elon bad etc etc

62400495
>>62400488
hello did you look at the chart recently?

62400827
>>62398185
Owari da

62400854
>>62375140

Haha

62401251
>>62375393
What exactly has he delivered?

62401264
>>62375393
you're stupid as fuck, spacex stock price has literally nothing to do with space

62401317
>>62400470
In my third world shithole the rightoid media (owners are musk allies) was shilling it on TV last friday
>not into trading stocks? this might be the perfect time to start, the world's first trillionaire bla bla bla
As if they would ever want plebs to get rich
Just an obvious pump and dump

62401616
>>62400470
i left a buy order for $160 by accident, my stock trading company has a thing where they cancel any buy order if its not fulfilled after like a week, so i forgot about it. It crashed today below 160 and i bought it when i didn't intend to. Oops, 5k pissed away on this overpriced stock lmao.

item.title: >boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasi… — 117 replies — 211.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasi…

Open thread • Replies: 117 • Updated: 2026-06-23 15:54 UTC

OP

>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago
>been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasing him
>haven't found any jobs, its unreal how bad the market is, have this bad feeling its never going to get better
>PIP ends on Tuesday
I am an engineer. I started off as a tech and worked my ass off getting bachelors full time to escape manual labor. Now im gonna be that goy with a masters degree who took a 500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work. I honestly feel like god did this beacuse its too much bad things happening at once that are culminating into my total demise.

Early replies (first 3)

62388691
>>62388676
>500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work
Trades pay more than ever, especially vs pajeet-saturated office work. Skill issue

62388694
>>62388676
My entire life has been nothing but fighting tooth and nail, working myself 1000x harder than anyone anywhere ever for 0.001% of what anyone else gets for free. I have felt that god is purposely shitting on me pretty much my whole life. It doesn't get better, it only ever gets worse. If anything happens to me that seem to be a good thing, I have 6 months max to enjoy it because that'll be taken from me and then I'll be beaten up and crushed down for added effect. It's a real comedy out there.

So don't worry bro, you have a lot of time ahead of you to suffer far more. If there really is a god, he must be the most perfectly evil of entities.

62388697
The worst part is that in my meeting with my boss today he didnt mention if I will pass the PIP. He said he needed to talk with some people including HR. So 90 percent chance he is going to fire me but just enough chance he wont that I have to spend the weekend waiting instead of processing the pain. And if he doesnt fire me, I will be a totally buckbroken employee who will never care about a rasie and work 60 hours weeks. Like a absurd housewife that has no were else to go and she tries to esapce and gets rapped(applies to ghost jobs) even worse so she comes back to him. Thats me. I used to think my relationship with work was like between a man and a woman, were I am the man that picks and chooses women and is the one who leaves but its all turned around past couple of years. I am nothing but a side pieace female whore to these companies now, they treat me like a contract worker without paying me like one. They got us with not giving raises to match inflation, they got us with return to work mandates and now, they are going to get us by firing us and leaving us with nothing. U will get put on a PIP too, at the worst time possible also. They wont even lay u off anymore, they want to save a little bit of money by denying us unemployment insurance by firing us through a PIP.


Was being in ur 30s a good excperince back in the day? There is no way human civilization would have gotten to were it is if it was always this bad for everyone. Of course, I am a faggot and just suck but the fsct of the matter is that PIPs and no jobs has been a very common trope on the internet lately

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62391513
>>62390249
>she still thinks satanists are in it for the edge alone
>thinking fascism is edgy, an authoritarian imposition of traditional morals

62391843
>>62389431
I'd welcome you to try, sukdeep dikshit. I'll break every bone in your body if you come at me with a saw. Have you ever even operated a saw?

62391855
>>62388729
>>62388736
Retards, mostly 2nd anon.

You get off of backbreaking shit after jman(4-6 years) and specialize. Or sales, or foreman, or estimators, or inspectors, or maint.

Low IQ stay in back breaking

62391907
>>62390573
No, you get unemployment only if you are laid off due to economic constraints or incompetence. Thats why PIPs aren’t that bad. You don’t get unemployment if you are fired for negligence (this can be fought if you have proof they set you up or if they were playing favorites) or if you leave willingly. Unemployment benefits tend to pay up to 2000 dollars a month depending on your salary.
>t. Corporate wagie who dealt with it before

62392212
>>62391843
And who might you be? How about you learn to speak only when spoken to.

62392253
>>62388711
>ah yes did i tell you? all women around me have fancy jobs with big salaries and 100% remote positions and company trips all over the world
the new system is in place permanently and working as intended

62392282
>>62388711
>but i refuse to do it because my gf would earn more than me
Yeah, that’s a very good reason. Never let a girl out earn you.
>t. retired at 25

62392283
>>62392253
> >ah yes did i tell you? all women around me have fancy jobs with big salaries and 100% remote positions and company trips all over the world
Doesn’t bother me. Retired at 25. I know how damaging a 9-5 is and how even waking up at 7am is shitty and terrible for your healthy. Going to bed with an alarm is terrible too. No girl is out earning me. Trust me.

62393076
>>62388802
>I see a nig or a nog here and there
Kek

62393091
>>62388992
Kys

I'm in a very comfy sport right now but most of my life has been a shitshow ripe with suffering. I'll be "grateful" when I'm dead. Until then it's hate, hate, hate and a lot of grit

62393160
>>62388730
May everyone you love die of cancer before your eyes.

62393171
>>62388761
Kys

62393407
>>62388802
This post PROUDLY presented by a jeet.

62393484
>>62391855
Ye bro, let me just causally go from doing interesting work to hammering in nails to build more houses for the jeet and beaner invaders that the boomers are gonna rent out to

62393491
>>62391907
Wait wait wai. I've been tearing my hair out cuz I dont think I will get bennies beacuse i am on a PIP and ur telling me i can still get them? I called the unemployment office and they said 50/50 so idk man. Would be nice

62393565
>>62393091
This is something I have to come to terms with as I get older. My luck isnt the worst b u t the people in my life, especially my biological family has been making.my life hell and I need to cut ties with them once my grandma dies. All the bad things that happened to me in life could have been way easier to deal with without my family making it worse.

62393627
>>62392212
silly esl. you spoke to me and I spoke back. glad you adjusted your energy to match your grip strength, bitch.

62394155
>>62388676
Is that Matteo or Matthew?

62395199
>>62393091
You should be grateful. If not for your suffering, you wouldn't be a successful man. Forgive your mother for making you think life should be like you are a woman.

62395202
>>62393160
I'll still be grateful for what they gave me. Learn to stop acting like an entitled woman. Karen.

62395616
>>62388778
>went to the worst liberal shithole of europe
>why am I struggling??

62395641
>>62388676
>people actually get put on PIPs
bro, how? I have so many workers in my department that don't try for shit at anything. literally nothing happens to them ever. corporate america has some fucked up shit, but generally I've seen the PIP system be less abused than other bullshit

62395717
>>62395641
Not every corporation runs the same
Shocking

62395927
My dream is, if my current gig works out (which will leave me with a lot of cash), is to start a software shop where the work-day is capped at 4 days. I don't care about making it big, I just want to spend my time at work with other people who are happy and live decent enough lives. Since it's an unexploited niche I wager I can gather some pretty decent talent.

Fuck this race to the bottom, I don't wanna be rich I wanna be happy

62395938
>>62395717
>>62395641

It's actually more about your boss. 90% of bosses don't gaf as long as you generally do what they say. There is a 10% of proactive dicks who are absolutely looking to roll heads.

62398374
>>62395927
Just give them wfh and treat them like human beings, engineers dont mind working overtime if its for the right person.


Also ye i had similar dreams to start a plesant working place even at the expense of profits but I mostly want to do it out of spite at this point

62399956
>>62388711
>ah yes did i tell you? all women around me have fancy jobs with big salaries and 100% remote positions and company trips all over the world
jewish daycare

62399988
>>62388694
I hold LINK too, brother

62400021
>>62388676
I was in a similar position like you OP. I disagreed with the staff engineer, he bad mouthed me to manager and I landed on a PIP. I spent 6 months on a PIP, looking for a job (I'm in Germany, they can't really fire me).
Mental was the hardest part. This is a process designed to break you down. What really helped me was to disengage with the work process, say nothing, do the bare minimum, wear my poker face.
After 6 months, I found a new job, negotiated an exit with a severance package.
I am not gonna lie, this was a shitty experience, but I learned a lot from it.

I wish you best of luck OP. Don't let them wear you down.

62401552
>>62388676
AI training is a decent stopgap while you're looking for work. Mercor and Micro1 are best, Handshake's okay, Outlier sucks but doesn't pull shenanigans with your pay (I've heard that Alignerr does).

item.title: KEK tradies are just as fucked by AI as white-collar fags. — 122 replies — 166.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
KEK tradies are just as fucked by AI as white-collar fags.

Open thread • Replies: 122 • Updated: 2026-06-23 15:43 UTC

OP

KEK tradies are just as fucked by AI as white-collar fags. Anthropic found a cheat code to dramatically accelerate robotics using LLMs.

https://www.anthropic.com/research/project-fetch-phase-two

Early replies (first 3)

62392639
So if we're all fucked, what's the endgoal?

62392654
>>62392639
Look what happened to horses

62392668
>>62392639
Cull

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62395154
>>62395010
There is no way that poster is real, he has to be AI agent talking to all the other ones on here (most of the "posters").

IS ANYONE REAL HERE? HENLO!?

62395201
>>62393713
Thts dumb as fuck. We already have premade buildings...you make them with cranes. No faggot robots needed. You make the crane automated...at your own peril.

62396828
>>62395154
Someone has to be real

62397022
>>62395010
yes, this is yet another balding discord tranny OCPD namenigger troll thread.

62397302
>>62392639
You will be left to eat cockroaches out of trash cans while the elites take your underage daughter back to their utopia to fuck and kill for pleasure. Oh, and you won't do shit about it either. Enjoy!

62397384
>>62395201
The world is sick of low iq blue collar workers.

62397402
>>62392639
the singularity
massive central processing warehouses where people will go to merge their consciousness with the computer

62397409
>>62395015
>I have access to self-hosted AGI too
May we see it? Your Claude mythos and blackwell systems?

62397444
>>62397302
They do. The masses are roleplaying being good but their nature is way darker than the elite's. That's the real
>infrared pill

62397478
>>62397444
The masses are low iq and poor.

62397486
>>62392639
pimp

62398293
>>62397478
Which is worse than being evil.

62398312
>>62392533
All the traders I talk to have fucked up knees and backs so not only are they just as worse off but they have fucked up bodies as well

62398353
>>62398293
My daddy always told me being poor is a crime

62398878
>>62392639
Dutch Disease but literally every country, especially the ones who invested the most in education.
Like how a petrostate does the bare minimum of investing in their own population and focuses 99% of their money on improving oil extraction.
AI countries will do the bare minimum of investing in their human populations, with 99% of their money going towards improving the AIs.
You can already kind of see this with the US and China. China gives its citizens jack shit but will happily invest in more robots. The US is sort of the same but more emphasis on thinking machines than robots.
Any country that doesn’t is going to be uncompetitive, though over time the AI dutch disease countries will also become uncompetitive in their own ways as the corruption and poverty rots them within.
In order to prevent mass poverty we’ll need to (in the short term) try to follow the norway model more than the typical petrostate model.
Like how Norway used its oil money to juice its sovereign wealth fund, we’ll need to use AI money on a sovereign wealth fund in order to keep our citizens from completely languishing away.
Long term we need to find ways to synergize human existence with machine existence. So that individuals can also benefit from the endless investment that will be put into the machines.

62398883
Oh look, another lie to prop up the stock price.

62398888
>>62392639
90% into the gulag they are literally unusable
Remaining 9% slave race because they happen to specialize in some field on par or greater to an LLM. They will serve the the remaining 1%

62398909
>>62392759
>>62394987
Why do anons always assume rich fags keep people alive? That they’ll just snap their fingers and everyone dies?
If the rich go for a segregated AI economy, then non AI using people will just continue to live on.
The “poors“ will even build AI robots of their own given enough time.

62398916
>>62393510
sure but how many humans are we talking
certainly not 8 gorillion

62398939
>>62398909
This is the first time in recorded history that a tool threatens with replacing humans as a mean of production. I know some of you dont understand the implications of this, but to pretend this scenario has happened before is retarded.
An AGI powered robot will be able to replace you, me and everyone else. You will not be able to develop your own AGI, because why would the elite give you means for independence? They are sociopaths, they only want you for your money, they are right now threatening you with your replacement telling you they want to replace you straight to your face.

62399166
>>62398939
>You will not be able to develop your own AGI
Kek, wanna bet on that?

62399211
blue collar is 4 losers

62399258
>>62393534
not him but the asian twink in the t-shirt, hes cute. probably has a 2m nw. would like to have sex with him.

62399375
>>62399166
Yes, i bet you don't even have the brain to finetune an open weights model, let alone develop something that still doesn't exist and will (probably) require a new computer hardware paradigm to exist.

62399432
Why can't finance fags understand economics ? It's not that robots can't do wagie shit, they absolutely can they just can't do it economically.

62401372
>>62392639
They'll exterminate us

62401385
>>62392654
>mr hands?

That makes no sense you weirdo pervert.

62401406
>>62393587
They check your prostate via blood tests exclusively now you raging faggot, god you can’t wait for robots to exist so you can shove one into your ass, you people are disgusting

62401454
>>62399432
put an indian brain in a robot's body

62401472
If (when) they can get it working properly and you have robots working 24/7 to build a house then tradies really are over

item.title: How the hell is the US still outpacing Europe for economic growth after everything that's happened? — 49 replies — 106.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
How the hell is the US still outpacing Europe for economic growth after everything that's happened?

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-06-23 16:00 UTC

OP

How the hell is the US still outpacing Europe for economic growth after everything that's happened?

Early replies (first 3)

62387242
>>62387239
Who cares

62387246
>>62387239
Wendeez nuts hit your chin

62387248
>>62387239
Because GDP has always been a meme.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62388389
>>62388374
Yes, it's true that modern US research unis are pretty good. However they are much better at publicizing their work than they are in actually doing novel work, in relative terms, compared to their EU peers. For example, pretty much all modern biology works on purely EU tech. But you will only read about what the US has contributed (or thinks it might contribute some day) if you look up information about biology advances.

62388394
>>62388389
I don't know as much about biology discovery pipelines admittedly, but I could see that and it makes sense.

62389689
>>62387318
> muh basic economic theory while ignoring consumer debt and wage stagnation

gdp numbers don't put food on the table for most people

62389710
>>62387388
> muh paperwork

europe's been coasting on welfare and red tape for decades, now they're shocked the brain drain is real.

62390217
>>62389710
Europe has been braindraining the US for a couple years now. The only reason it had gone the other way until now has been that the US is flooded with free money going around (ironic then that you blame welfare!) for business activities and salaries. But this has largely equalized for salaries (not quite there yet but you could say it's there if you add the work conditions and life conditions in the US have greatly deteriorated and many are no longer willing to get the salary difference to put up with it). It hasn't yet equalized for funding though, but it's become just hard enough to raise a business instead of a scam in the US that people who want to actually make real businesses don't feel the need to go to the US anymore (more like, US investors no longer invite them in, as it were).

62390244
>>62388089
Man you really drank the russian propaganda.

62390247
>>62387239
Is it really though? Or is it all just stock market inflation money bubble?

62390270
>>62390247
>statistics aren't real
>>62390244
>russia made them
>europe is actually doing really well economically just not by any measurable metric

62390282
>>62390270
gdp is well accepted to not be real in any way, yes.

62390345
>>62387239
>took over the empire
>work harder and more
>have more resources, more land
Truly a mystery.

62390485
>>62387239
Wealth is fake and gay

62390633
>>62387239
Because as much of a shitshow as this country is, everywhere else is somehow even worse

62390680
>>62387239
Because the US is still the place for people who want to make it.

Europe is just fucking stupid for regulations. Whatever you want to do you can't, and if you can it'll be fucking expensive because energy is stupidly high thanks to a bunch of globohomo regulations.

Also most Europeans have a shitty "good enough" mentality, probably because the government cucks them when they make it and has so many gibs it's barely worth trying. A Frenchie wakes up, has a croissant and cafe, goes to the book store at 10 to work until noon, takes an hour for lunch with wine, cheese, stew, works until 4, has more wine and food with friends and calls it a day. They live in a shit flat and rely on the government for basically everything (even their lunch is subsidized) but whatever

62390706
Theyre gay and take too many holidays

62393701
>>62390217
Is there any actual proof that more Americans are moving to Europe than the other way around?

62393779
>> Europe
What do you have, like ten companies of any concern out of your whole crumbling continent?

62394192
>>62390282
What's real then? Your schizo delusions?

62394541
>>62387300
Can we bribe them to do their job?
Would that work????

62394554
The us is pumping more oil and gas than Russia or saudis Arabia

Europe is importing almost all its energy

62394607
>>62387239
Numbers are fake like the Chinese ones.

62394679
>>62387239
>after everything that's happened
what happened? are you retarded?
and how is it hard to outpace europe? europe does nothing

62394688
>>62387239
>How the hell is the US still outpacing Europe for economic growth after everything that's happened?

communist leaders bet on austerity... win stupid prizes

62394698
>>62394688
>communist leaders bet on austerity

Except they didn't, they just spent all their money on stupid shit like windmills and refugee housing programs. The same thing that's happening to canada.

62397412
>>62394679
The tariffs? The strait closing? A bunch of military spending moving away from the US into Europe?

62397491
1) US dollar is still the king of the world especially in cross border settlements.

2)Petro dollar

62401448
>>62387239
The US stock markets is bigger than the next 9 combined!
Go fuck your self yuropoor

62401516
starting a business in europe in general is hell if it isn’t a sole proprietorship. the amount of capital needed to go from 0 to 1 employees is daunting. it’s honestly incredible that there are any European startups at all.

62401538
>>62387239
>economic growth!
Cool, that just means some billionaire is making more money.
You shouldn't downplay where Europe is far ahead, namely in the quality of life, the quality of public services including healthcare and transport, the cleanliness and walkability of cities, food quality, etc etc.
Don't you just love eating chlorinate poisoned chicken because some kike farmer can't be bothered to pay for clean pens?

62401575
>>62387404
>because 2/3 of their GDP dollar value comes from AI or AI related industry
Lmao, that's not how GDP works at all. The top 10 companies for contributing to it are:
1. Amazon
2. Walmart
3. United Health Group
4. Apple
5. Alphabet
6. CVS Health
7. Berkshire Hathaway
8. McKesson
9. Exxon Mobil
10. Cencora
Google, okay fine, we'll call that AI. I'll even give you Amazon just for shits and giggles. That's 2 of our top 10. Europe is poor because they strangle every business in the crib that isn't some 300 year dinosaur.

62401581
>>62387388
>all their researchers are in america
You mean pajeets?

62400853

62400886

62400889

These are the voyages of the bagholder: Space X. It’s continuing mission, to seek out new exit liquidity and new top buyers. To boldly dump on everyone who bought before.

62400925
>>62400838

I hope it goes to $20 because I hate Elon Musk and people who like him.

62400926
>>62400838
Trust the plan

62400959
>>62400838
AAAAAAAAAAAAA
CAN WE START PANICKING NOW?
EVERYTHING I HAVE IS IN THE RED
IS THIS THE TOP SIGNAL
ID IT TIME TO SELL OFF?
I DONT WANNA BE LFET HOLDING THE BAGS
AAAAAAAAAAAA

62400966

62400985
>>62400966

62400988
>>62400889

62400989
>>62400838

62401068
FUCK MY STOP LOSSES TRIGGERED

62401075

62401108
>>62401075

Because the whole thing is market manipulation.

62401115
spacex to zero

62401116
>>62401115

One can only hope.

62401148
it's not even below IPO price you little babies

62401151
>>62400838

it's like that thing we told you was going to happen actually happened

62401197
>>62400985
kind of ironic, if we bail out those niggers, this will be the commie shit to do.

62401470
>>62400838
>pyramid pattern
waagmi

62401551
>>62401470
Cybertruck pattern

62401619
>>62400838
oh no is Grok suddenly not worth >2 trillion dollars?

62401656
>>62401470
Huh? thats it? thats what people are panicking about? Even my dad said this wasn't a buy until 120 dollars. I was thinking it was already somewhere around 80 or 90 you people need to learn to toughen up.

item.title: I'm trapped in a tutorial level in life. — 20 replies — 80.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'm trapped in a tutorial level in life.

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-23 16:27 UTC

OP

I'm trapped in a tutorial level in life.

>zero work but deadend WFH job
>no social life at all, too ugly to get a date and too autist to fit in anywhere
>in my 30s and missed out on all 20s socialising
>live in Britain and watch and listen to stories of people getting rich in America
>go on holidays and just walk about, never talk to anyone, visiting museums
>financially, saving money makes most sense, but haven't saved enough to not have to work, don't earn enough to spend money on anything interesting
>no technical skills, no expertise, doing anything would take huge amounts of effort and likely lead to no outcome

I am a spectator in this world.

Early replies (first 3)

62398493
>>62398482
The older I get the more I don't understand how more people haven't gotten violent about the state of things. It's extremely bleak out there.

62398521
>>62398482
>doing anything would take huge amounts of effort and likely lead to no outcome
well yeah, that's life. start by expending some effort to figure out what you want to do, what interests you. then continue expending effort doing that thing

62398532
I’ve had a very similar life but I’m American and 42. I did work in an office but have been remote since the lockdowns started. only difference really is I got to save and invest.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398608
>>62398493
Life is easier than ever. You are so rich you have time to cry on an incel blog about imaginary problems

62398688
>>62398482
>>62398493
>>62398532
White harder.

62398732
>>62398608
Meanwhile in reality where everyone is rich and everything is easier than ever:
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=6lFXmDk-tps

62398861
>>62398732
It is easier to get rich, but losers like you dont even try you just look for excuses and cry on incel blogs while posting government media to soothe yourself

62398903
>>62398608
>Life is easier than ever. You are so rich you have time to cry on an incel blog
sure
>about imaginary problems
ok you got me, i thought your post was serious for a sec.

62398924
>>62398903
OK chicken little, here's an example>>62398732 this idiot is worried about birth rates when he himself is a poor incel crying his life away. Go outside and take what you want all your competition is scared losers crying in their basement

62398956
>>62398493
I already have lol, and will escalate it more and more

62399052
>>62398956
Enjoy prison, tard.

62399065
>>62398493
OP barely has to work and has housing and is able to take vacations and you think that is bleak?

I was reading about what life was like in China in the 70s and it was common for the children to have lice and get frostbite in the winters, you whiny babies are so priveleged and yet you just want more more more.

62399814
>>62399065
>our problems are diminutive because third world bug people got frostbitten
This is the same as crying about niggers in africa starving, who fucking cares? The West is so advanced because White people made it so, that doesn't mean problems Westerns face in the West aren't actual problems.

62401167
>>62398521
This. Nothing good comes easy to normal people. You have to put in the work to earn it.

You should pick some goals, even 1 to start then break it down into achievable, smaller goals.

Eg you want to be beautiful (however that isn't measurable so first make it a measurable goal) then get some feedback from doctors eg in Korea to determine what procedures you would need and how much they cost and how long that will take you to save up for, plus what order you can do them in. And what can you do in the meantime eg around tongue posture.

Do the same for your body and diet.

Start trying things that make you uncomfortable eg scuba diving or rock climbing, things that push you a bit.

You're so deep into a "woe is me" cycle that you think everything is pointless but the truth is you haven't tried enough things to have any idea what is even possible and enjoyable for you. You have enough money to start loving your life, so get to it.

62401546
>>62398482
>I am a spectator in this world.

There are 8.6 billion people here doofus we are all spectators. You think everyone is just living it up and leaving you behind specifically. Get a hobby and make yourself interesting.

62401561
Stop making this thread you dumb nigger, drink bleach instead.

62401610
>>62399065
It sounds like what OP really wants is some friends and maybe a gf/wife family. It’s really not too much to ask for and it’s also not surprising that not having these things makes him discontent.

The problem is that everything today is so easy, that things that require work are more difficult than ever. You can’t push a button and have a friend. It takes work and human connection which are in short supply today. Don’t even get me started on women and dating.

His complaints and criticisms are valid.

62401620
>>62398482
start squatting heavy and invest in tqqq. let me guess? you need more.

62401633
>>62398482
This is my exact situation, I'm from the same country too.

Did you have an easy middle class upbringing too?
Did your parents have enough money to make life easy so you never developed any toughness, but also not enough money or the inclination to send you to a private school so you could be moulded into a refined person?
Did you spend a lot of your childhood playing runescape?
I'm just wondering.

62401699
>>62399052

Prison was OK Idc if I go again

item.title: What made you still hold BTC? — 102 replies — 55.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
What made you still hold BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 102 • Updated: 2026-06-23 14:37 UTC

OP

Bitcoin has gone through multiple cycles, crashes, and endless criticism, yet many continue to hold. What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

Early replies (first 3)

62371733
>>62371718
>What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

62371807
The only reason to ever sell bitcoin is to buy more at a lower price.

62371917
>>62371807
correct
>waiting for a good entry

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62387549
>>62383732
If you buy memecoins, not $btc

62387563
>>62387534
Defi is no longer valuable

62387566
Im fine

62387833
>>62387563
Depends what you mean by valuable. A lot of the hype is gone, but people are still borrowing, lending, and farming yield.

62387836
>>62387549
Doge coins lol

62387875
>>62371718
Because it's the only crypto asset I'd be comfortable holding for the next decade without constantly checking the chart.

62387892
the longer this market exists, the more Bitcoin seems to become the benchmark everything else gets compared against. So very very bullish

62388642
>>62387875
100%

62388704
>>62371718
Institutions are starting to make it into BTC, before bridge was the issue but with TBV eliminating that, more money will start flowing into BTC, it still feels early.

62388780
>>62376610
> printing is the only thing keeping the bubble from popping instantly. you want that?

62388819
>>62377444
> nothing has changed except the price chart and your cope levels

62388854
nothing, im not retarded so i take profits. my cost basis is negative now.

62389149
>>62388780
You know my answer for that

62389340
I feel like something new has to happen before the market shifts sentiment.

All the ETFs and government regulation just hurt the project.
Nobody is utilizing the tools, the tools still feel beta, and developers are being arrested.

If it's just going to be a big gay digital rock the momentum grinds to a halt.

The problem is some of us got rich and have something to lose now.
Different factions formed inside the developer community.
Is there incentive to free ball the network still? idk.

I'm still holding but this bear feels different

62390164
>>62388819
So how will you change it?

62391118
>>62389340
I'm still holding as well.

62391156
>>62371718
It feels like shit when it goes down but when you're near the bottom like now you just remember the last times you felt this way and how it gongo up from here, everytime. Bobos are ngmi. Mumu supremacy is real. Cope trannies.

62391165
>>62383619
Based retard

62391192
>>62371718
The 20% long-term-capital-gains tax rate that I'd have to pay if I cashed out all at once.

62391471
The only investments I hold are schizo nonsense Chainlink and PMs. I have a good amount of money through my Roth and 401k. I can only invest in specific etfs through my work 401k. What’s the best method to get long term Bitcoin exposure through a IRA?

62392524
>>62391156
You're not a real investor

62393205
>>62372380
Then how come I can send BTC to my Bitcoin core wallet dumbfuck?

62393451
>>62371718
I believe in the philosophy that led to bitcoin. Sadly the vast majority of humanity are just cattle. I still hold because I believe there is something that can happen. The pessimism on this board doesn't change my view on this. I will hold and if it fails I'll be ok because I have stocks and silver and land.

62395366
>>62391192
That's too much to be honest

62395566
>>62371718
I plan on selling when I retire in ten years. But who knows maybe I will change my mind and keep holding.

62396187
All these retards are greedy, that's all there is to it

62398052
>>62395566
Keep going anon

62399672
>>62396187
Oh really huh?

62399688
>>62371718
I forgot the passwords for one account and everything got stolen from the other.

62401169
>>62399688
Story maker

item.title: Polkadot update — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot update

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-23 16:27 UTC

OP

ITS HERE

XRP, ETHEREUM AND LINK IS COOKED

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

https://news.openshore.io/web3-summit-2026-recap.html

Early replies (first 3)

62399118
5. Privacy is a Competitive Advantage
In a world of surveillance capitalism, Polkadot's emphasis on privacy-by-default and user agency differentiates it sharply from other ecosystems. The proof-of-personhood protocol and private payment demonstrations showed concrete technical approaches, not just rhetoric.

Do they think big Google and Facebook will let them get away with this?

62399184
After further research

It seems we have a fork of consensus

https://forum.polkadot.network/t/introducing-a-new-jam-token/13029

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

So what the fuck is JAM and how will it bring value back to DOT marketcap?

62399190
>The JAM protocol presupposes the existence of a token to ensure soundness in its state usage. I argue that DOT would make a highly suboptimal choice and that a new, fixed-issuance token to represent the new Polkadot resource of JAM state makes the most sense. Assuming DOT DAO were to launch a Polkadot JAM for its upgrade, then this new resource-access token would be wholly owned by DOT DAO by default. DOT utility would continue as at present, implying control of DOT DAO — and with it, effective ownership of the Polkadot JAM — as well as the means for staking, collateral and coretime payment within the Polkadot ecosystem.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62399197
>>62399190
oh boy, another scam

62399251
Slurped

62399395
>>62399197

Linkie crying seeing another crypto vibing

62400350
>>62399184
a new resource is being introduced to Polkadot with the JAM upgrade (fast shared storage like RAM) and you need one JAMKB per KB of RAM you want to use on the network, so you can't buy more tokens than there are KBs of RAM

62400419
>>62399084
DOT will make us all rich beyond our Wildest dreams!!

62401678
>>62399084
I'll believe they're serious when I see this on an fdroid repository

62401689
>>62401678
they had a grapheneOS build for the event so it is likely that will happen

62401700
>>62399197
Cope

item.title: I genuinely do not undertsand the plan with SPCX The money they got from the IPO was already blown on data centers which… — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I genuinely do not undertsand the plan with SPCX The money they got from the IPO was already blown on data centers which…

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-23 16:22 UTC

OP

I genuinely do not undertsand the plan with SPCX
The money they got from the IPO was already blown on data centers which are going to depreciate instantly, and they've come out and said they're going to need more
The stock is already collapsing and will be worth less than a hundred dollars before lock ups even end, so how do they plan to raise money for more data centers? They won't be able to IPO for $75b again since that'll be more than the company is worth

Early replies (first 3)

62401023
>>62401012
Americans pension money

62401029
>>62401023
The NASDAQ 100 is not popular enough to keep the stock afloat in an open trading market. If it were the S&P 500 I would agree, but they have no interest in the stock until after a year of price discovery

62401296
>>62401023
>Americans pension money
and thats good, they voted for it!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62401485
>>62401012
They have infinite future money from space satellites, asteroid mining, moon healthcare centers, and space shipping so they can afford to blow it on data centers.

62401507
>>62401012
>so how do they plan to raise money for more data centers?
memes aside, they are obviously hoping for more government contracts to bail them out. spacex makes almost all of their money from the government

62401524
>>62401012
What's hard to understand? The plan is to extract as much money as possible from the public during the peak of SpaceX hype

62401533
>>62401507
That fat fuckwit is gonna LOVE the next Democratic Government of the US of A. Sieg Heil them, you fat fucking bellend.

62401563
>>62401533
I don't think there is a single Democrat in the presidential conversation who's interested in tanking their popularity by siding with Elon. Democratic voters despise him

62401640
>>62401012
Elon owes money to a very powerful man from Saudi Arabia and will end up in a snuff video if he doesn't deliver

62401671
Sorry newfag here, isn't building data centers waaaay more profitable than building space exploration technologies? I don't really see the issue here.

62401679
>>62401671
Literally no one has made money doing it

item.title: >ETH foundation just fired everybody What's the cope now, ETHcuckolds? — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>ETH foundation just fired everybody What's the cope now, ETHcuckolds?

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-23 16:20 UTC

OP

>ETH foundation just fired everybody
What's the cope now, ETHcuckolds? It's over. I laughed at your dumbasses faces every time people put the king BTC and shitcoin ETH together like they were worth the same. The house of cards is finally collapsing.

Early replies (first 3)

62401156
>>62401146
Well BTC would fire its staff but the NSA are federal employees

62401161
>>62401146
Where the fuck is my sub 1500 eth? Releze ze quantum fud kindermaxikings!

62401166
>>62401156
Laughs in Kekpstein

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62401215
>>62401146
based
20% less selling
homomorphic encryption, keccak-256 upgrades, and other software developments can be achieved with ai

62401346
>>62401146

62401396
>>62401146
Beneath the flickering neon lights of a decaying metropolis, acid rain corroded the steel of the skyscrapers.
A man shivered in a dark alley, his stomach empty, his glassy eyes lost in misery.
Vitalik appeared, a spectral figure floating above the pools of hydrocarbons, a digital halo around him.
Without a word, he touched the wretched man's trembling retinal interface, hacking through the surrounding chaos.
A silent transfer occurred within the infinite flow of the blockchain, a golden light piercing the darkness.

"Just one token," he murmured, his voice echoing like source code sacrificed to fallen humanity.
The beggar's balance instantly shifted from zero to obscene wealth, annihilating his hunger and cold. The street's automated systems bowed, suddenly opening the doors to an unexpected synthetic comfort. Vitalik vanished, dissolving into the source code of this nightmarish city, leaving behind a miracle.

In this world of shadows, hope had just been written in a few bytes, changing a destiny in a fraction of a second.

62401461
Well their foundation site looks a lot more professional now and similar to what an open source project site should be. Basic, to the point, light weight.
Their older site looked like some girl's tumblr page with animation and colors and auto playing videos etc. So maybe their team really was full of faggots and women wasting oxygen.

62401475
>>62401396
do you know da gwei?

62401659
>>62401146
too low shouldve been 120%

62401674
>>62401146
eth is the only defi chain that is actually used

item.title: only trips can decide the fate of SPCX — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
only trips can decide the fate of SPCX

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-23 16:14 UTC

OP

62401028

item.title: Why should I max out a Roth IRA and not just put 20% in that and 80% into buying calls 6+ months out and/or selling puts… — 20 replies — 15.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why should I max out a Roth IRA and not just put 20% in that and 80% into buying calls 6+ months out and/or selling puts…

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-23 15:08 UTC

OP

Why should I max out a Roth IRA and not just put 20% in that and 80% into buying calls 6+ months out and/or selling puts?

Early replies (first 3)

62394825
You should buy 0DTE OTM options

62394847
>>62394796
This is AI. Even people who do free climbing like this don't (can't) climb on smooth surfaces for that length.

62394859
>>62394847
Its not ai. Its a French freeclimber Alexis landing.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62394874
Because you don't know what you're doing and your expected rate of return into options plays will be lower than whatever safe shit is in your IRA.

62394882
>>62394874
I made 33% last year only selling puts otm. Much better than my 457b.

62394884
>>62394882
Oh cool. I guess that one year metric means you're good to go and will on average make more money than your IRA. Go for it!

62394897
>>62394884
I guess you're right. Its hard to see when you've only won. I only do it on profitable and safer companies like WMT and NVO etc. As well but any company can have massive downsides. I just want to retire before I'm well past my prime • •

62394934
>>62394796
Looks like a big garloid mating with a small garloid

62394944
>>62394897
All I'm saying is don't get too carried away with gambling based on recent historical results. It's probably fine to be a bit more aggressive than the average normie but a good amount of shmucks with the mindset you have think they are invincible and end up losing half their net worth in 5 years. Just be careful anon.

62394995
>>62394847
looks like there's a few videos of this guy as >>62394859 says. might be legit

62395174
>>62394796
Options are a zero sum game. For you to win, someone else has to lose, and you are up against people who are the best in the world at it and have vast computing and research assets at their disposal. It is hard to beat the forever tax-free gains of the Roth IRA/401k.

62395208
>>62394847
>>62394859
I love it when arrogant faggots get immediately buttblasted

62395712
You can just put the calls in your roth and have no tax on gains

62395776
>>62394796
Aww hell nawww.

62395792
>>62394796
man these videos always make my balls get sweaty

62399430
>>62394796
uuh

62400290
>>62394796
that video makes me ill

62400323
>>62394796
Just imagine while you're climbing you suddenly need to take a shit.

62401247
>>62394847
>this is fake because I don't think anyone would do that
shut the fuck up you retarded faggot https://www.fb.com/watch/?v=1720307272184817

62401310
That's nothing compared to holding chainlink

item.title: Easy 30% a year — 8 replies — 7.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Easy 30% a year

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-23 15:26 UTC

OP

Why can't you just hold S&P 500 above the 200 SMA with 2x leverage to avoid liquidation and get double the gains of the S&P every year, headache free?

Early replies (first 3)

62393790
>>62393727
>Why can't you
But you can.

62393835
because if everyone knows about your super secret trading strategy they'll copy for themselves and it wont play out the way you think it. retard.

62394008
>>62393727
A (((glitch))) will most likely liquidate (you) and there will be no refunds.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62397322
>>62394008
Antisemite!!!! MODS MODS MODS HEA A TERRORIST HE WANTS TO HOLOCAUST ME! MOOOOODS!!!

62397461
Let me guess....youve only been "investing" since late 2022.

62397572
>>62397322
You need to be at least 18 to post on this website.

62397663
>>62393727
you still have to pay for margin interest costs for the 2x leverage. all the midwits would tell you that its not as good as a roth IRA holding VOO.

62401381
>>62393727
>with 2x leverage
with what, an ETF? You get ETF decay. With futures? Contango losses.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 138 replies — 65.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 138 • Updated: 2026-06-23 14:20 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62393801
Please keep all stock suggestions relevant to the discussion at hand

62393834
>>62392256
the same but at least im locked in. If I lose my job tomorrow and never invest another penny I'll still be fine to retire well before 65. that's peace of mind. 10 more years and my rentals will all be paid off and that will more than cover my existence costs.

>>62393449
>just make money
>just buy assets
yeah that's how 99.9% of everyone with actual money got it. biz loves to talk about how they made x billion on cumcoin yet in the real world thats akin to winning the lotto

62394075
LASR
IPGP
PLAB
QUBT
RGTI
IONQ
Nbis
IREN
AAOI
POET
SIVEF
SOITEC
SMHN
CRDO
ALMU
LASE
OPTX
SKYT


Some stock picks to look into in the world of photonics

62394345
>>62394075
>NBIS ($286.69)

-Too expensive for us

>CRDO ($271.83)

-Too expensive

>AAOI ($161.85)

-Too expensive

>SOITEC (Euro stock, $133.39)

-Too expensive

>IPGP ($118.01)

-IPG Photonics primary supplier of high-power fiber lasers and automated laser systems heavily utilized in Elon Musks TESLAs

>LASR ($64.73)

- **Laser Photonics (LASE)** – Develops industrial laser systems and is expanding into **anti-drone technology**."
>IREN ($59.96)

-Not relevant, disregard


>IONQ ($56.55)

-Moreso quantum related, not really photonics

>SKYT ($36.57)

-Recently bought by IONQ,


>RGTI ($21.36)

>>Reddit says:
-the most speculative, revenue is actually declining and the P/S is hard to defend at any timeline, the honest framing for all of them is these are binary outcome bets not compounders. fault tolerant quantum is 5-15 years out, they'll keep burning cash and diluting until then.

>POET ($12.15)

Pump ended

>QUBT ($10.76)

-They make the tlfn photonic chip. Where silicon based chips use electrons, their tfln chips use
photons to send information. First tfln chip foundry in the U.S.


>SIVEF ($10.12)

-Fidelity only, pushing to get US LISTING


>LASE ($2.32)

-We missed this pump

>OPTX ($12.11)

- We missed this pump

LYTE - Releasing August, from the same company that brought you DRAM.

FOTO - this is the ETF for photonics tickers, very over diversified however

62394373
>>62373752
Is there a market for books with alternative physics?

62394789
The third topic shall be future tech and science new

62394870
>>62392312

Go learn how to create a validator.

PulseChain validators right now are raking in around 10%. It's nice.

62394942
>>62394345
>too expensive
anon, fractional shares exist

62395063
>>62373752
>I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.
Yes, going to college or vocational school and getting a higher paying job is probably the correct path for the vast majority of you.
>t. 32yo engineer making $135k
Go for STEM and if you can't cut it then pick a trade that interests you. If I didn't make it in STEM I was considering being an Air Traffic Controller.

62395577
Brainstorming

Software created current-state AI, AI created current state demand for hardware resources, hardware surge created demand for production materials, hardware costs contribute to higher cost datacentre build costs, running the datacentres with current state AI running on current state hardware consumes higher energy demand.

>AI - priced in
>Hardware - priced in
>Materials - priced in
>Datacentres - priced in
>Energy - priced in

So the disruptor to this ecosystem might be sitting where we started - software development. The race to highly energy-efficient AI models is the next logical thing. The current trajectory is unsustainable from a resources perspective, and the financial burn rate is unsustainable.

You make AI consumption highly resource efficient, all of those things come down as the demand reduces. Or does supply just increase to fill the increased capacity?

That takes me to the next innovative era that will consume the increased capacity;
> Robotics and humanoids / androids - building interest but probably not fully priced in yet
> Transport and automated movement - AI powered, driverless vehicles (short Uber)
> Highly efficient reusable rocketry - transport anything anywhere quickly (reusability powered by AI robotics)
> Personal digital self's - Consumer facing AI-version-of-you running your life, in your house, ordering your groceries - your life on autopilot (think a personal JARVIS)
> medical application - localised AI models in hospitals and medical rooms - control robotics in surgery
> mobile devices - uncertain future, at some point the personal handheld device model may come to an end

So my take away for a macro, strategic tech play could be to accumulate software and tech players with resources that can produce highly efficient AI models that use less resources - Microsoft is trading at a discount? - instead of over-committing to hardware and the fundamentals. Model efficiency could improve at any stage.

Thoughts?

62395589
>>62394870
Don’t you need like 7 figs worth of pulsechain to be a validator

62396733
>>62377801
Why would you want those with over 10K to leave? It sounds like you could use some advice especially if you're five figures and "investing" in trading cards. Your finest minds have more than 10K because they minimally are employed with decent jobs.

62398741
>>62395577
I agree with your thesis that we should accumulate up and coming software players with great ai

that said

> Robotics and humanoids / androids - building interest but probably not fully priced in yet

we're too early

> Transport and automated movement - AI powered, driverless vehicles (short Uber)

https://www.azfamily.com/2026/01/08/waymo-passenger-flees-after-car-drives-phoenix-light-rail-tracks/

I live in AZ, everyone hates Waymo. Uber drivers will literally bully the crap out of them


>Highly efficient reusable rocketry - transport anything anywhere quickly (reusability powered by AI robotics)

Too broad

>Personal digital self's - Consumer facing AI-version-of-you running your life, in your house, ordering your groceries - your life on autopilot (think a personal JARVIS)

This has to be inevitable by the end of the next decade. The writing is on the wall, I mean think of Siri and Alexa, different in scope but has easily prepared consumers to accept the eventuality of "you" just running your own tasks. Small business owners would also love this. Imagine a traveler who stays at an Airbnb month long for business and uploads his AI to manage the boring stuff of his new "home" while he acclimates to the business and travel of the area.


>medical application - localised AI models in hospitals and medical rooms - control robotics in surgery

this has potential

>mobile devices - uncertain future, at some point the personal handheld device model may come to an end

We're too early

62398767
>>62398741
https://www.eurekalert.org/news-releases/1132599

>Publishing in the Journal of the American Medical Association (JAMA) online June 22, the study team found that online portal messages more than doubled between 2020 and 2025 (by 153 percent). By contrast, total telephone calls decreased by 6 percent over the same period.

>“Clinical staff will need to be trained in mastering the tools of messaging in healthcare; in using AI support programs, including chatbots that can frame content to minimize its complexity; and in making the most effective use of clinician time needed for online billing and online counseling,” said Dr. Segev, who is also a profession in NYU Grossman’s Department of Population Health.

>Already, he noted, NYU Langone uses AI support tools to speed up drafting of physician and provider notes.


Doctors already use google to diagnose people

62398789
Some topical reading for today, gentleman, nothing too difficult takes less than 5 mins. Lets take this slow and not bite off more than we can chew

62398798
>>62396733
They'd have no reason to frequent and appreciate these threads or this company. You think rich boomers understand our circumstances?

62398799
Is diversification a meme? Thinking it's better to just go all in on a single stock than an index fund when you have a small account.

62398806
Page 2, come on anon, it doesn't hurt to learn the basics, i'll go over with you, together

62398809
>>62398799
How much are you putting down? I'm thinking of doing $1000 all in picks and making a folder that way

62398813
There, lets read over these three

62398868
>>62398799
You are better off getting a job

62398876
>>62398868
https://voca.ro/1aI6fGYlNRNN

62398905
>>62394942
Don’t get that either. Even if you only bought full shares how could you not afford 200 dollars worth of shares? Is this under 10k net worth or under 100$ net worth

62398985
>>62376699
> you're arguing with a script and losing

62399017
>>62380691
bump

62399272
>>62398789
>Wavelength (λ) (THE SYMBOL IN HALF LIFE)

Distance between repeating points of a wave (e.g., crest to crest). Determines frequency and, for light, color.

>Amplitude:

Maximum height of the wave. Determines intensity (brightness for light, loudness for sound).

>Phase

Position of a wave within its cycle. Determines how waves add or cancel when combined.

>Polarization

Direction of oscillation of a transverse wave. For light, it is the orientation of the electric field.

62399358
Tomorrow we shall give predictive programming a try as photonics will likely be a difficult venture

62399394
10k- networth reporting in! You guys are too smart for me with all those grand ideas.

62400367
>>62398789
tl;dr

62401110
7 figure club. I already lost more than 10k today at market open. Stay poor fags
